dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All four requirements are satisfied: no flash on normal page load, warning appears correctly when localStorage is blocked after mount, StorageWarning in app/page.tsx continues to work, and no hydration mismatch (SSR and initial client render both see storageOk=true).

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: isAvailable() wraps localStorage access in try/catch and the useEffect correctly propagates the false result. The existing writeStore quota-exceeded path is unaffected. Minor: a theoretical race where writeStore sets saveAvailable=false before the hook first renders is handled correctly because available = storageOk && saveAvailable still evaluates to false after mount.

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: The flash is eliminated for normal users. Safari private-mode users see the warning after mount. The dismiss button continues to work. No regressions to interactive elements.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: The eslint-disable-next-line comment references 'react-hooks/set-state-in-effect', which is not a real rule in the standard eslint-plugin-react-hooks package. The comment is harmless (disabling a non-existent rule is a no-op) but adds cosmetic noise. The pattern of useState(true)+useEffect is idiomatic and correct for SSR-safe initialization.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Three new tests verify the core behaviors: initial-render optimistic state, available-after-mount, and unavailable-after-mount. All 218 existing tests continue to pass. A minor gap: no test verifies the module-level saveAvailable interaction (quota-exceeded path), but this path existed before the PR and is covered indirectly.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No security surface is introduced or changed. No API keys, no user input flows through the modified code.

issues.description: The eslint-disable comment references 'react-hooks/set-state-in-effect', which is not a real rule in eslint-plugin-react-hooks. The actual plugin only defines 'react-hooks/rules-of-hooks' and 'react-hooks/exhaustive-deps'. The comment is harmless (a no-op disable for a non-existent rule) but is misleading and adds noise.
issues.fix_guidance: Remove the eslint-disable comment entirely — it serves no purpose. setState inside useEffect is valid React; there is no real lint rule being suppressed here.

summary: The fix is minimal, correct, and well-tested. Changing useState(() => isAvailable()) to useState(true) + useEffect eliminates the SSR/hydration mismatch that caused the flash banner. Both SSR and the initial client render see storageOk=true (no warning), and the useEffect fires after mount to set the real value. All 4 task requirements are met. All 218 existing tests pass plus 3 new hook tests covering the SSR-safe initial state, the available case, and the blocked-storage case. The only finding is a P2: a cosmetic eslint-disable comment that references a non-existent rule.
